textPlain: What level of PM2.5 are they considering? I cannot read the full study
Only so much you can do about ambient pollution in your city.Looking carefully at your cooking situation is worthwhile though. Was horrified by the spike in readings from stuff like steak in a pan

I've heard the argument made that it's better to stop burning fossil fuel, even if you're a climate change denier for reasons like this.Even if you think climate change is a hoax, why not reduce pollution anyway?
Therefore city dwellers are demented and rural dwellers aren't.(And this says nothing about the effects of noise pollution or aesthetic pollution)

An air purifier is pretty cheap and will cut down on indoor pollution from cooking a lot. Or at least that’s how it seemed from the somewhat unreliable sensor I was using.https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-air-purifier...
Are there studies showing indoor air pollution from cooking in a residential setting is linked to dementia?
Well studies like this are often directed at policymakers, who actually can do something about it.

I agree. It also reduces the world's dependence on unstable regions.However, some things that are regarded as renewable (e.g. wood burning, such as the notorious Drax power station in the UK) are more polluting than fossil fuels. Personally I doubt they are even effective at CO₂ reduction. What we need is clean energy.
